summary: CARE UK SOCIAL CARE LIMITED (LEI: 213800DBE5Y9ZM58PN63; GB Companies House number: 07068789) is identifier-confirmed as a United Kingdom-registered entity, verified across GLEIF, OpenCorporates, and associated identifiers. Two board appointments — JORGE MANRIQUE CHARRO and KHALID AHMED HAYAT — and a senior managing official, CSC CORPORATE SERVICES (UK) LIMITED, are recorded from 2024-10-01 per OpenCorporates. GLEIF data discloses a GLEIF reporting exception whereby an unknown parent entity does not consolidate the subject, resulting in a medium-confidence opaque ownership signal: the beneficial ownership chain contains an anonymous entity and cannot be fully traced. Additionally, a high-confidence structural risk signal has been raised because the ownership chain reaches into jurisdictions outside the EU/EEA (specifically GB), a condition relevant under AMLA CDD RTS criterion (b). No material gaps were identified in the evidence packet, though the OpenAleph and UK Companies House sources were consulted and no additional findings were returned from those sources.

claims.text: CARE UK SOCIAL CARE LIMITED is identifier-confirmed as a United Kingdom-registered entity, with LEI 213800DBE5Y9ZM58PN63 and GB Companies House number 07068789.
JORGE MANRIQUE CHARRO holds a board appointment in CARE UK SOCIAL CARE LIMITED from 2024-10-01, per OpenCorporates.
KHALID AHMED HAYAT holds a board appointment in CARE UK SOCIAL CARE LIMITED from 2024-10-01, per OpenCorporates.
CSC CORPORATE SERVICES (UK) LIMITED holds a senior managing official role in CARE UK SOCIAL CARE LIMITED from 2024-10-01, per OpenCorporates.

GLEIF data records a reporting exception whereby an unknown parent entity does not consolidate CARE UK SOCIAL CARE LIMITED, meaning the beneficial ownership chain contains an anonymous entity and cannot be fully traced.
A medium-confidence opaque ownership structural risk signal has been raised, derived from GLEIF data, because the ownership chain contains an anonymous entity.

A high-confidence structural risk signal has been raised because the ownership chain reaches into jurisdictions outside the EU/EEA (specifically GB), a condition relevant under AMLA CDD RTS criterion (b), per OpenCorporates.

limitations: No beneficial owner could be identified: GLEIF records a reporting exception whereby an unknown parent does not consolidate the subject, leaving the ultimate ownership chain opaque (f1, f2, r1).
OpenAleph and UK Companies House were consulted but returned no additional findings recorded in the evidence packet.
